headline: Intelligence Operation in a Box
orientation: Exploratory design (Mar 16 2026) argues that collapsing build cost enables any intelligence workflow to be instantiated as a configurable tenant on shared infrastructure. The River provides a communal raw‑data stream; Managed Operations (the “box”) consume, process, and optionally contribute to the permanent Wire Graph market. A 21‑stage GNE editorial pipeline is presented as a template that can be re‑configured for policy, market, scientific, personal, or investigative use cases. The codebase already supports multi‑tenancy; the remaining work is to make pipeline, stage orchestration, delivery, Wire policy, and River subscriptions data‑driven. A full reset (“Nuke”) of the current GNE instance is required before the template can be validated by running a clean end‑to‑end daily edition with Wire contributions emitted only at publish time.

topics.current: Build cost collapse allows an intelligence operation to be deployed as a configuration on shared infrastructure. Newsbleach is the first instance, showing that the same machinery can become a policy shop, market intel desk, scientific monitor, etc. The Wire Graph is the substrate where processed intelligence lives permanently; the River is a shared ingest commons of raw items that never touch the Wire.
topics.name: Thesis & Core Concept

topics.current: Three zones: River (shared ingest commons), Managed Operations (Intelligence‑in‑a‑Box), and Wire Graph (market). The River streams RSS, APIs, legislative trackers, patent databases, arXiv, scrapers, etc. Everyone can contribute sources and fish items. Managed Operations are multi‑tenant, configurable pipelines that select topics, processing rules, delivery methods, and contribution policies. The Wire Graph stores permanent intelligence with provenance, reputation, pricing, and adversarial survival signals.
topics.name: Three Zones Architecture

topics.current: The current Newsbleach pipeline has 21 stages grouped into four phases: Collection (stages 1‑3), Research & Enrichment (4‑6), Adversarial Processing (7‑14), Edition Assembly & Delivery (15‑21). Each stage is mapped to a generic intelligence role (e.g., River polling, Signal extraction, Quality gate, Presentation). Auxiliary stages include Source Candidate Evaluation, Compute Reliability, Source Cartography, and a 4‑stage Digest Pipeline.
topics.name: GNE Pipeline Mapping

topics.current: Purpose: monitor legislative feeds and produce policy briefs. River sources: Congressional Record, Federal Register, state legislature feeds, court filings, regulatory announcements, think‑tank blogs. Pipeline: phases 1‑2 unchanged; phase 3 uses policy analysts, structured brief format, bias‑checking adversarial layer; phase 4 delivers alert‑style emails, no hero images. Wire contribution: 7‑day embargo for first‑mover advantage. Uses 16 of 21 stages (skips layout, hero image, front‑page polish, arc detection, adds alert trigger).
topics.name: Configuration A – Policy Shop

topics.current: Purpose: track competitor moves, product launches, funding, partnerships. River sources: Crunchbase API, SEC filings, company blogs, GitHub, patents, trade publications, earnings calls. Pipeline: heavy filtering in phase 1; phase 3 uses market analysts, competitive intelligence reports; phase 4 weekly digest with optional hero image, market‑moves table. Wire contribution: sanitized summaries with embargo for subscribers. Uses 14 of 21 stages (most edition assembly omitted, digest pipeline added).
topics.name: Configuration B – Market Intelligence Desk

topics.current: Purpose: monitor new papers and breakthroughs. River sources: arXiv, bioRxiv, medRxiv, PubMed, Google Scholar alerts, lab blogs, conference feeds. Pipeline: novelty‑weighted evaluation; phase 3 uses research analysts, research summaries (methods, findings, significance, limitations); phase 4 daily roundup with breakthrough alerts. Wire contribution: open‑access, no embargo. Uses 15 of 21 stages.
topics.name: Configuration C – Scientific Research Monitor

topics.current: Purpose: single‑user customized feed. River sources: user‑chosen feeds plus private corpus. Pipeline: personal relevance scoring; minimal adversarial processing (user is editor); push notifications for high‑signal items, daily email digest for others; no edition assembly. Wire contribution optional. Uses 8 of 21 stages (lightest configuration).
topics.name: Configuration D – Personal Intelligence Agent

topics.current: Purpose: deep multi‑week investigation on a specific topic. River sources: targeted feeds, manual tips. Pipeline: narrow filter; maximum adversarial processing with multiple independent threads, cross‑verification, timeline reconstruction, entity relationship mapping, heavy arc detection. Delivery: evolving investigation dashboard, final publication upon conclusion. Wire contribution: full investigation with sourcing. Uses all 21 stages plus custom evidence‑tracking stages.
topics.name: Configuration E – Investigative Slow‑Burn

topics.current: Existing pipeline supports multi‑tenancy via `publication_id`. To become a reusable template, the following changes are required: (1) store editorial constitution, persona definitions, stage selection, and delivery prefs as per‑tenant data; (2) make stage orchestration configurable instead of a fixed 21‑stage sequence in `triggers.ts`; (3) allow pluggable delivery mechanisms (email, web, API, push); (4) configure Wire contribution policy per tenant (embargo, pricing, contribution vs private); (5) configure River subscription per tenant (topic filters, source categories, custom sources). The main gaps are GNE‑specific constitution/persona definitions and the assumption that all 21 stages always run.
topics.name: Codebase Implications

topics.current: Before any templating work, the current GNE instance must be reset (“Nuke”) to clear stale greenlit items and drafts that cause pipeline hangs. See `newsbleach-three-layer-architecture.md` for the reset procedure. First milestone after the Nuke: GNE runs cleanly end‑to‑end, produces a daily edition, and emits Wire contributions only at publish time, proving the template works for its first configuration.
decisions: decision: reset GNE instance before templating
decision: milestone – clean end‑to‑end daily edition with Wire contribution at publish
topics.name: Nuke & Milestone
